quoteFirst: At intervals he gesticulated and muttered a phrase.  He was making excellent progress with it and at five and
quoteLast: he was at the end of it.  The prince sat stolidly in the easy chair by the long windows.  At twenty-four minutes to three the baron flung out the last damning phrase (with the appropriate splendid gesture) at his image in the looking-glass over the mantelpiece.
title: Happy Pollyooly: The Rich Little Poor Girl
author: Jepson, Edgar, 1863-1938; Birch, Reginald B. (Reginald Bathurst), 1856-1943 [Illustrator]

quoteFirst: A fine snow pelted my face.  I stopped at the first lamp and pulled out my watch.  It was
quoteLast: .  What if the time-tables had been changed?  A prayer rose to my lips; there was so much in the balance.  Down this street I ran, rounding this corner and that.  I knocked down a drunken student, who cursed me as he rolled into the gutter.  I never turned, but kept on.
title: Arms and the Woman
author: MacGrath, Harold, 1871-1932

quoteFirst: The next naval victory was won by Captain William Bainbridge, this time in command of the Constitution, forty-four guns, over the British thirty-eight-gun frigate Java, Captain Henry Lambert. The battle began at
quoteLast: , and at 4.05 p. m., the British frigate was "an unmanageable wreck." The Java at length surrendered, having lost sixty killed, besides one hundred and one wounded, while the loss of the Constitution was nine killed and twenty-five wounded.
